node.title: Will Recruiters Consider 1 Year of Internship Experience for Full Time Software Engineer Roles

article.title: Will Recruiters Consider 1 Year of Internship Experience for Full Time Software Engineer Roles
article.content:
Hey everyone,
I could really use some guidance.

I’ve been working as a Software Engineer Trainee (Apprentice) for the past 1 year, and my tech stack includes Java, Spring Boot, Spring Security, React.js, Node.js, Docker, Kubernetes, and AWS. I’ve consistently performed well, taken ownership, and put in my 100%. I was genuinely expecting a full-time conversion… but instead, I’ve been given a 6-month extension of my apprenticeship.

This has honestly shaken my confidence a bit, and now I’m actively exploring full-time SWE/Backend Engineer roles outside. Many job descriptions mention 6 Months–2 years of experience, and I’m confused about whether my “Apprenticeship” experience will hold the same weight as a standard full-time role.

My questions:

Will recruiters consider 1 year of Apprentice/Trainee experience as valid experience when applying for full-time roles requiring 1–2 years?

What’s the best way to present or pitch my experience so recruiters see the value?

Any tips on how to convince recruiters that I can fully handle a standard Software Engineer role despite the apprentice title?

I’ve worked on real projects, handled production issues, and contributed across the stack. But I’m unsure how the title might be perceived. I'm decent at DSA as well

Would really appreciate advice from anyone who’s been in a similar situation or has experience with hiring.

Thanks! 🙏

post.content: If its at a legitimate company definitely just consider it as yoe, do not have any worries.
